extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AVERMAET takes yellow as BMC wins 35km TTT by 4 seconds over Sky, capitalizing on winter-long R&D investment and four course reconnaissances.
tactical_patterns: BMC's December R&D commitment and four course previews (including three in one day) proved decisive over Sky's raw horsepower.
Sky deliberately ceded 3 seconds to avoid yellow jersey media/protocol burden until rest day after stage nine.
VANGARDEN took massive final 200m pull with only five riders remaining, revealing BMC's depth management strategy through attrition.

rider_signals.VANGARDEN TJ: positive: Led BMC's winning effort with massive closing pull, confirmed world-class ITT form from California tour
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: Strong TTT performance suggests GC form despite previous doubts about team support quality
rider_signals.CRADDOCK Lawson: positive: Heroic TTT contribution with broken shoulder blade exceeded warmup-based expectations, took actual pulls for URAN
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Already 50+ seconds down on GC before mountains, Sky tactically satisfied despite time loss
rider_signals.SMITH Dion: positive: Looked strongest on his team during TTT despite holding polka dots, suggesting genuine climbing form
